skill.name: planning-with-files
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to use persistent markdown files for planning, notes, progress tracking, and final deliverables during complex work.
skill.summary: Use markdown files as durable working memory for complex tasks.
skill.author: AmmarCodes

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution patterns, weak-crypto indicators, a browser-storage reference, reconnaissance indicators, and one hardcoded URL. Manual review found these are markdown examples and documentation text, not executable code or instructions to exfiltrate data. The skill does encourage creating, reading, and editing local markdown files, so the main residual risk is expected filesystem use during workflow planning.

low_findings.title: Filesystem-Oriented Workflow Instructions
low_findings.description: The skill intentionally asks the assistant to create, read, write, and edit local markdown files such as task_plan.md and notes.md. This is core functionality and not malicious, but users should expect filesystem changes in the working directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source repeatedly instructs the assistant to use local markdown files for planning and notes. The behavior is explicit and legitimate for the skill purpose, with no evidence of unsafe file targets.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Mistaken for Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many fenced blocks as Ruby or shell backtick execution. Manual review shows they are human-readable workflow examples such as Read task_plan.md and Write notes.md, not executable scripts or command substitution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside markdown documentation blocks and names assistant tool-like operations. No executable code file, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction was found.

low_findings.title: External URL Used as Source Citation
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URL is a citation to Manus context engineering documentation. It does not instruct the assistant to send data to the URL or make an automatic network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is under a Source heading and is presented as reference material. There is no nearby credential access, upload instruction, or automated request logic.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto and Browser-Storage Alerts Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak-cryptography and browser-storage alerts come from prose and example planning notes, including markdown filename references and a dark mode example mentioning localStorage. No hashing implementation or sensitive browser data access is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are embedded in markdown documentation and an illustrative UI planning example. I found no cryptographic operation, no credential handling, and no instruction to inspect real browser storage.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive: Fenced Workflow Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: Fenced blocks labeled bash or markdown describe assistant workflow operations and plan templates. They are not executable shell scripts and do not contain command chaining, downloads, or arbitrary code execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The patterns are markdown examples showing reads and writes to task files. They lack executable syntax that would run system commands in this repository.

content.user_title: Plan Complex Work with Markdown Files
content.value_statement: Long tasks can lose context, decisions, and progress over many tool calls. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a file-based workflow for plans, notes, and deliverables.
actual_capabilities: Creates a task_plan.md structure for complex multi-step work.
Defines phases, status, key questions, decisions, and error logs.
Encourages notes.md for research findings and source summaries.
Uses repeated plan reads to keep goals visible during long tasks.
Separates planning notes from final deliverable files.
Documents error recovery instead of silent retries.
limitations: It is a workflow guide, not an automated project manager.
It may create extra markdown files in the current workspace.
It does not validate source accuracy or run project tests by itself.
It is excessive for simple questions and small single-step edits.

use_cases.title: Manage Research Projects
use_cases.description: Use a plan file and notes file to track sources, findings, open questions, and final synthesis.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Step Coding Tasks
use_cases.description: Break bug fixes or features into phases, record decisions, and keep progress visible across tool calls.

use_cases.title: Produce Structured Deliverables
use_cases.description: Maintain working notes separately from the final report, design brief, or implementation summary.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a moderate task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the planning-with-files workflow for this task. Create task_plan.md with phases, key questions, and current status before starting.

prompt_templates.title: Track Research Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use task_plan.md and notes.md while researching this topic. Save source findings in notes.md and update the plan after each phase.

prompt_templates.title: Manage a Coding Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Feature work or bug fixing across several files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan this coding task with task_plan.md. Track root cause, decisions, files touched, errors, verification steps, and the final implementation summary.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Long Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced work with many tool calls and changing context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the full three-file pattern. Keep task_plan.md current, store research in notes.md, create a deliverable file, and re-read the plan before major decisions.

output_examples.input: Research a market and prepare a concise summary.
output: A task plan with research, synthesis, and delivery phases.
A notes file containing sources, key findings, and open questions.
A final summary file that references the researched findings.

output_examples.input: Fix a bug that spans several modules.
output: A plan showing investigation, root cause, implementation, and verification phases.
A decision log explaining which files and functions were changed.
An error log that records failed attempts and resolutions.
Separate working notes and final deliverable content.
Updated task status after each major phase.
A closing summary with completed work and remaining risks.

best_practices: Create task_plan.md before starting any task with several phases.
Re-read the plan before major decisions and update it after completed phases.
Keep notes, decisions, errors, and final deliverables in separate sections or files.
anti_patterns: Using the workflow for quick questions that do not need persistent state.
Writing large research results only in chat instead of saving notes.
Retrying failed actions without recording the error and resolution.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to manage complex work with markdown plan, notes, and deliverable files.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or install software?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files are markdown instructions and examples, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Will it modify my workspace?
faq.answer: Yes. Its normal workflow creates or edits markdown planning files in the working directory.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for research, coding changes, project planning, and tasks that span multiple tool calls.

faq.question: When should I skip it?
faq.answer: Skip it for simple questions, quick lookups, and small single-file edits.

faq.question: Does it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
